name: Brian Heidik
signature_move: Pioneered the 'goat strategy' by maintaining separate Final Two deals with Helen, Ted, and Clay, then systematically eliminating each ally in order while dragging Clay — the least likable player — to the end as a beatable opponent.
strategy_summary: Brian treated Survivor as a cold business transaction, forming calculated alliances purely for utility and discarding each one at the optimal moment. He maintained total vote control from start to finish — never once receiving a vote against him — while engineering every post-merge elimination. His ruthless efficiency won the game but alienated the jury so deeply that he barely scraped by with a 4-3 vote, with Ted Rogers famously calling it a choice between 'the lesser of two evils.'
fun_fact: Before Survivor, Brian worked as a used car salesman, which his castmates frequently cited as the perfect metaphor for his gameplay — he could sell anyone on anything, but nobody felt good about the deal afterward.
